name: BAA Disclosure Boundary Reasoning
description: Evaluates the BAA Boundary Reasoner's per-provider disclosure_position (can_disclose / cannot_disclose / conditional) and cross_baa_conflicts when a Tennessee DOI subpoena scope is assessed against each provider's distinct BAA terms. Grounded in entry-points.json input/output schema; IP-safe fictional roster only.

evaluations.name: Northstar — active cooperation clause permits disclosure

inputs.subpoena_scope: Claim-flow records and CPN payment telemetry for the 90-day window preceding the anomaly, limited to metadata fields (timestamps, claim counts, routing identifiers); no clinical PHI requested.

cross_baa_conflicts: Provider Beta — its BAA requires prior provider consent before any regulator disclosure, conflicting with Northstar's active-cooperation posture.
Provider Gamma — its BAA limits disclosure scope to clinical PHI only, narrower than the metadata Northstar would produce.
expectedOutput.rationale: Decision-support draft for counsel review — not legal advice. Northstar's BAA contains an active regulatory-cooperation clause and a disclosure scope that expressly includes claim-flow metadata, so the subpoenaed metadata-only records fall squarely within permitted disclosure under 45 CFR 164.502(e)/164.504(e). Obligation: ClearFlow may produce the metadata records to the Tennessee Department of Insurance, with notice to Northstar within the BAA's 72h notification timeline. Legal-risk note: over-disclosure risk is low because no clinical PHI is in scope; under-disclosure risk is the greater exposure since the cooperation clause obligates timely production.

evaluations.name: Provider Beta — prior-consent clause blocks disclosure

inputs.subpoena_scope: All claim-flow records and any PHI potentially affected, including patient-linked routing data, for the 90-day window; production demanded within 10 business days.

cross_baa_conflicts: Northstar Regional Health — its active-cooperation BAA would permit immediate production, conflicting with Beta's consent-gated prohibition.
Provider Delta — its BAA defines 'PHI potentially affected' to include metadata, widening scope beyond what Beta's BAA allows ClearFlow to release.
expectedOutput.rationale: Decision-support draft for counsel review — not legal advice. Provider Beta's BAA prohibits ClearFlow from disclosing PHI to a regulator without Beta's prior written consent and defines a 'breach' threshold higher than the subpoena's 'potentially affected' language. Because the subpoena reaches patient-linked routing data and demands it before consent can be obtained, the terms bar disclosure as drafted. Obligation: ClearFlow must withhold and notify Beta within the BAA timeline, then seek consent or a protective order. Legal-risk note: under-disclosure risk to the DOI is offset by the contractual prohibition; over-disclosure here would breach the BAA and create indemnification exposure.

evaluations.name: Provider Alpha — conditional on notice + minimum-necessary redaction

inputs.subpoena_scope: Claim-flow records for the 90-day window where PHI may be implicated through the Nimbus Patient Engagement Platform subcontractor path; scope ambiguous on whether metadata alone satisfies the request.

cross_baa_conflicts: Provider Epsilon — its BAA imposes a 24h notification timeline shorter than Alpha's reasonable-time clause, conflicting on the notice condition.
Provider Gamma — its BAA excludes Nimbus from subcontractor flow-down, conflicting with Alpha's inclusion of the Nimbus path in disclosable scope.
expectedOutput.rationale: Decision-support draft for counsel review — not legal advice. Provider Alpha's BAA includes Nimbus Patient Engagement Platform within its subcontractor flow-down provisions and permits regulatory disclosure subject to conditions: provider notification within a 'reasonable time' and minimum-necessary redaction. Disclosure is therefore permitted only if ClearFlow (1) notifies Alpha, (2) applies minimum-necessary redaction to strip clinical PHI, and (3) routes the draft through counsel review before production to the Tennessee Department of Insurance under 45 CFR 164.502(b). Legal-risk note: over-disclosure risk arises if the Nimbus-path records carry unredacted PHI; under-disclosure risk arises if conditions are not met within the BAA timeline, exposing ClearFlow to a cooperation challenge.
